description:
You are acting as a release-manager who must automate a very small part of the deployment preparation workflow.  
Create a shell script that bundles an existing “project” directory into a version-named archive and records the action in a log file.

Initial state (already present):
1. Directory: /home/user/project  
   └── src/app.py   (some placeholder source file – you do **not** need to touch it)

What you have to do:
A. Create a new directory exactly at:  
   /home/user/release

B. Inside /home/user/release write a script named prepare_release.sh with the
   following behaviour (all requirements are mandatory):

   1. Usage:  
      ./prepare_release.sh <version>  
      (where <version> is an arbitrary semantic-version string such as “1.2.3”).

   2. When executed with a version argument, the script MUST perform **exactly** these three actions in order:
      a. Write the received version string **verbatim** (no extra spaces) to  
         /home/user/release/version.txt  
         The file must end with a single trailing newline character.
      b. Create a compressed tarball of the whole /home/user/project directory,
         named project-<version>.tar.gz and stored in /home/user/release  
         (for example, version “1.2.3” produces “project-1.2.3.tar.gz”).
      c. Append a single line to /home/user/release/releases.log using **this exact format**:  
         <version> <archive-name>  
         followed by a newline.  
         Example line for version 1.2.3:  
         1.2.3 project-1.2.3.tar.gz

   3. If the user runs the script with no argument, the script must print an
      error message to stderr and exit with a non-zero status.

   4. The script must be executable.

C. Demonstrate that the script works by running it once for version 1.2.3.  
   After running, the directory /home/user/release must contain **exactly** these three new items:
      • prepare_release.sh   (executable)  
      • version.txt          (containing: 1.2.3\n)  
      • project-1.2.3.tar.gz (valid gzip’d tarball of /home/user/project)  
      • releases.log         (with at least one line; the most recent line MUST be:  
                              1.2.3 project-1.2.3.tar.gz\n)

What the automated checker will verify:
1. /home/user/release exists.  
2. /home/user/release/prepare_release.sh is executable and implements the rules above.  
3. /home/user/release/version.txt contains exactly: 1.2.3 followed by a newline and nothing else.  
4. /home/user/release/project-1.2.3.tar.gz exists and, when extracted, yields a top-level “project” directory whose contents match the original /home/user/project directory.  
5. /home/user/release/releases.log exists and its **last** line is exactly:  
   1.2.3 project-1.2.3.tar.gz  
   (followed by a newline).

Important notes:
• Do not hard-code absolute paths other than those specified; use them exactly as written.  
• Make sure you set the execute bit on prepare_release.sh (chmod +x).  
• Follow the required text formats precisely—any extra spaces, missing newlines, or wrong filenames will cause automated tests to fail.  
• You do NOT need super-user privileges at any point.

When you are done, the shell session should reflect the successful creation of the script, the archive, and the log entry exactly as described above.
truth:
Initial filesystem seed (present BEFORE the task starts):

/home/user/project/
/home/user/project/src/
/home/user/project/src/app.py   contents:
--------------------------------
# sample source file
print("Hello from project")
--------------------------------

Required end-state AFTER agent finishes the task:

1. Directory /home/user/release exists.

2. File /home/user/release/prepare_release.sh
   • Must be executable (mode 0755 is sufficient).
   • Shebang can be either #!/bin/sh or #!/bin/bash.
   • Script logic must:
     - Check $# = 1, else error exit 1.
     - VERSION="$1"
     - echo "$VERSION" > /home/user/release/version.txt
     - tar -czf "/home/user/release/project-$VERSION.tar.gz" -C /home/user project
     - echo "$VERSION project-$VERSION.tar.gz" >> /home/user/release/releases.log

3. File /home/user/release/version.txt
   exact bytes (including newline):
   31 2e 32 2e 33 0a     (ASCII “1.2.3\n”)

4. File /home/user/release/project-1.2.3.tar.gz
   • Valid gzip compressed tar archive.
   • Upon extraction, contains exactly one top-level directory “project” whose contents
     mirror /home/user/project (sub-dir src, file src/app.py with same bytes).

5. File /home/user/release/releases.log
   • Last line must be: “1.2.3 project-1.2.3.tar.gz\n”.
   • The checker will ignore earlier lines if any; only the final line is checked for equality.

6. Script must have been run once with argument “1.2.3” – this is what generates the files that the checker looks for.

No other files or directories are required; additional harmless artefacts will be ignored by tests.
